item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: Aaand I just read the declassified and now I'm an even bigger fan. Great stuff.

Very interesting article. I like the ideas in it.
I think there's a bit of a disconnect or a bit of confusion as to how its discovery in a previous cycle is relevant - is most of the article written in the previous timeline or just the discovery logs? I was a little confused as to which context I was meant to be reading most of this under.
Regardless, this poses a good mystery, it's got a good enough hook to keep you going all the way through a fairly long article, and I think I'm going to reread it to see what else I pick up on. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
now the bad thing about this SCP is it forces lot of detail into you

You could say that again. *wink*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Coincidental! Never played that game.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Any further discoveries related to GoI-5008 ("The Keepers") are to be censored and compartmentalized under the same restrictions as this file. SCP-5008 is to be considered available for this purpose, but conventional information suppression tactics are in place as part of the operational profile of MTF Alpha-1 ("Red Right Hand").

Should circumstances require it, unauthorized personnel are to be led to believe that there is no SCP-5008. Outside of Site-01, SCP-5008 is not to be referred to except when absolutely necessary, and then only as "HUSH," its approved codename.

The Keepers

HUSH

Binding of Isaac references: deliberate or coincidental?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, I have no idea what I've just read.

item.title: Re: A question for the author
item.content:encoded: Goddammit… thanks.

item.title: Re: A question for the author
item.content:encoded: At the beginning of the second paragraph of conprocs, there's still a reference to Beta-7, despite the change to Gamma-7 in the first paragraph.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Late reply, but (in my view, at least) merely mentioning a character or group that has a tag in an article is not sufficient to justify using that same tag. The Fifthists and the Sarkics actually have nothing directly to do with this device. And who knows, maybe that letter refers to a different Dr. Light!

item.title: Re: Holy fuck
item.content:encoded: Right at the end, at the very bottom, below the forward/backward SCP links, click on the right-pointing arrow.

item.title: Re: A question for the author
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the catch. While there is actually no issue with contradictions between articles, I like to avoid it if possible.

item.title: A question for the author
item.content:encoded:
I have a question for the author and a possible request to edit something. The first line in the Special Containment Procedures section The site of SCP-5008-Prime is physically contained by MTF Beta-7 ("Burning Coronet") references a task force designated as Beta 7 with a nickname of ("Burning Coronet").

However, there is already a task force with that designation with a different nickname in the SCP Foundation and the other task force has with a different type of role. These are the MTF Beta-7 ("Maz Hatters") who specializes in the acquisition and containment of anomalies exhibiting extreme biological, chemical, or radiological hazards as well as the rapid containment and cleanup of areas affected by such anomalies.

So, for the sake of continuity, I would suggest changing the task force containing your SCP to a newly created one that deals with containing your specific SCP. I would recommend one similar to MTF Eta-10 ("See No Evil") who deal with the initial containment of cognitohazards.

Of course, if I missed something about that particular part of your article, I would love to be corrected. Haha regardless, this was a really well-done SCP.
Nice!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An elegant answer to a very complex question. I like this a lot +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i felt myself getting bored in the addenda, which had little to keep me interested in what was going on. the clef note was a decent breather because it broke the monotonous tone, which paired with the pacing issues, completely killed any interest i had in this piece. it felt like it dragged on for far longer than it should have.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this one on the whole. The device is awesome, and I really liked your characterization of the Erikeshian civilization. Really, the only issues I had with this piece was a minor bit of clunky pacing and some unanswered questions about those mentioned "irregularities", but those weren't enough to kill my fun. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
at "Archaeological Site 231", I immediately went O.o I'm honestly a little surprised you're not requesting 5231, heh.

I was not disappointed as I continued to read. this was fun and engaging. it was a bit long, but i barely noticed; the time flew by and i was almost sad at the end that it was over.

now to find that hidden collapsible…

item.title: Re: Holy fuck
item.content:encoded: Wait, I think I found it using page source.

item.title: Re: Holy fuck
item.content:encoded:
Would you be willing to guide me to the hidden collapsible.

I wasn't able to find it, probably because it's hidden so well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Finally something talking about the Erikesh

Edit: Also, should the Sarkic, Fifthist, and Dr Light tags be added? I also feel like there are more references to other skips beyond 2000.

item.title: Re: Holy fuck
item.content:encoded: Aha! Thanks!

item.title: Holy fuck
item.content:encoded: Commenting again because I just found the hidden collapsible and holy shit. Holy shit. Holy shit. That was absolutely chilling, and recontextualized a lot of the article for me. I think I like it even more now if that’s possible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a good one since it gives a lot of detail and slowly pulls you into reading more and there is not lack of creativity (Data redacted,Data expunged etc.) now the bad thing about this SCP is it forces lot of detail into you and that is not a bad thing for someone used to reading long SCPs but a new person wouldn't want to read it. Overall this is a good SCP [+1]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the praise! Have a look at the tags if you're curious about the sisters. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really good but I don't understand the last file, about the Berkshire sisters. They weren't mentioned before; are they from another SCP?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Goddamn, this is good. It’s beautifully formatted and written, and answers a question I never really knew I had. There has to be times when amnestics aren’t enough, after all, times when too many people have seen something they shouldn’t. What does the Foundation do then? This is what. Not to mention, the backstory is intriguing and thought-provoking, and that last addendum leaves just enough up to interpretation to make me want more. Easy +1.

item.title: Author Post - rattles finally posts a new skip
item.content:encoded:
Silence will fall when the question is asked…

So, how is it that a paragovernmental organization can have unlimited funding, hundreds if not thousands of massive research and/or prison facilities, diplomatic ties to every national government, agents in every police force and media outlet in existence, space programs beyond count, and a literally apocalyptic human rights record, and yet remain totally unknown to the public?

With thanks to...
With thanks to:
  • Rounderhouse, Woedenaz, Croquembouche and djkaktus, all of whom designed elements of the formatting I've used
  • Lord_of_Laugh, Panteradactyl, Oboebandgeek99, GerrymanderBassist, T Rutherford, and DrAkimoto for crit at various stages
Images
Images

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Ruins_of_Chefren_(1878)_-_TIMEA.jpg
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Acelerador_de_Particula_1996.jpg
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Amsbeamtesting.JPG

Now with a declass by swordlover87.
